D5 Design L3 Apply Atom

Dyslexia-Friendly Tints

stark black on pure white can shimmer for some readers — a soft cream or pastel tint and dark-grey ink calm the page

For readers with dyslexia or visual stress (Meares-Irlen), maximum black-on-white contrast can make text appear to shimmer, swirl, or crowd. Guidance from the British Dyslexia Association is to avoid pure white backgrounds and pure black text: a low-saturation cream or pastel tint with dark-grey ink reduces the glare without losing legibility.

Step through the tints. The passage stays the same dark-grey ink; only the page colour changes. There is no single best tint — preference is individual — but moving off stark white is the common first step.
